topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The air in the room felt thick, suffocating. Every time I glanced over, a cold knot tightened in my stomach. It started subtly—a misplaced pen, a book left open on my desk—but then I saw it. A page from my private journal, tucked carelessly beneath my textbook. My breath hitched, a sharp, involuntary intake of air. I felt a surge of intense resentment, hot and acidic, rising in my chest. I stood up abruptly, the scrape of my chair against the floor sounding deafening in the silence. When my roommate, Mark, walked in, his casual demeanor instantly shifted to one of mild surprise. "What's up?" he asked, his tone light, completely missing the tremor in my hands. I didn't say anything. I just stared at him, my jaw clenched so tight I could feel the muscles protesting. The silence stretched, heavy with unspoken accusations.

[story 2]

It was the sheer audacity of it that made my skin crawl. I found the evidence hidden inside my notebook, the ink of my deepest, most unguarded thoughts laid bare across the pages. A wave of pure, corrosive anger washed over me, making my vision swim slightly. I felt a desperate need to lash out, to shatter whatever fragile peace we shared. When I confronted Mark, my voice came out surprisingly steady, though I could feel the vibration of fury thrumming beneath my skin. "Why are you reading my stuff?" The question was sharp, edged with something brittle. Mark just blinked, a slow, uncomprehending movement. He offered a weak, apologetic shrug, a gesture that felt like a deliberate dismissal of everything I felt. I turned away quickly, the heat in my face burning, and retreated to my side of the room, meticulously rearranging my belongings as if trying to impose order on a chaotic world.

[story 3]

The discovery hit me like a physical blow. My stomach twisted into a tight, nauseous coil. Looking at the pages, seeing my vulnerabilities exposed under his gaze, sparked a furious, internal combustion. Every nerve ending seemed to scream for me to lash out, to make him understand the sheer trespass of his actions. I didn't raise my voice; instead, I lowered it, making it dangerously quiet. "You shouldn't have looked," I managed, the words tasting like ash. I watched his reaction closely, searching for any flicker of remorse, but there was only a blankness in his eyes. A deep, simmering dissatisfaction took root, settling in my core like poison. I picked up my own journal, flipping through the pages with exaggerated slowness, a silent, pointed judgment aimed squarely at the person who had violated my trust.
